task_completion.description: Verify the 'bug' tag has been customized with the correct color and icon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'bug' tag pill in the interface. It should have a red background (hex #d73a4a) and display a warning/alert icon next to the text.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'enhancement' tag has been customized with the correct color and icon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an 'enhancement' tag pill in the interface. It should have a light blue background (hex #a2eeef) and display a filled star icon next to the text.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'documentation' tag has been customized with the correct color and icon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'documentation' tag pill in the interface. It should have a solid blue background (hex #0075ca) and display an info-button ('i') icon next to the text.

task_completion.description: Verify the Triage Dashboard has been added to the sidebar.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the right-hand sidebar menu. There should be a new tab labeled 'Triage' alongside the default tabs (like Open, Recent, Tools, etc.).

task_completion.description: Verify the dashboard contains the 'Critical Bugs' section with listed tiddlers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the 'Triage' sidebar tab (or the 'Triage Dashboard' tiddler if open), look for a text header exactly matching 'Critical Bugs', followed by a list of tiddler links.

task_completion.description: Verify the dashboard contains the 'Enhancements' and 'Documentation' sections with listed tiddlers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the 'Triage' sidebar tab (or the 'Triage Dashboard' tiddler if open), look for text headers exactly matching 'Enhancements' and 'Documentation', each followed by their respective lists of tiddler links.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the TiddlyWiki graphical interface to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the TiddlyWiki UI being interacted with, such as the Tag Manager, tiddler edit mode, or adding fields like 'caption' and 'tags'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not bypass the UI by using terminal commands to inject the dashboard or tag styling.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not show commands like 'curl', 'wget', or direct file manipulations of the node.js tiddlers directory to complete the core task.

integrity.description: Ensure the changes are actually saved and rendered in the application, not just left in draft mode.
integrity.visual_evidence: The dashboard and tag pills should be visible in their final rendered state (e.g., the sidebar tab is active and clickable), not just as raw wikitext in an unsaved draft tiddler.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 'bug' tag must have background color '#d73a4a' (red) and icon '$:/core/images/warning'. The 'enhancement' tag must have background color '#a2eeef' (light blue) and icon '$:/core/images/star-filled'. The 'documentation' tag must have background color '#0075ca' (blue) and icon '$:/core/images/info-button'. The dashboard tiddler must be titled 'Triage Dashboard', tagged with '$:/tags/SideBar', and have a caption field set to 'Triage' (which makes it appear as 'Triage' in the sidebar tabs).
